36	## Code style
37	
38	- Python 3.12+. No type stubs or mypy. Use type hints where they aid readability.
39	- Dollar amounts in results.md: round to nearest penny (`.2f`).
40	- Durations in results.md: always in minutes with 1 decimal (`{seconds/60:.1f}min`).
41	- No emojis in code or docs unless the user asks.

43	## Repository rules
44	
45	- **No agent-generated `.github/workflows/` at repo root.** Agent workflows only exist inside workspaces under `workspaces/`. The repo's own CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) is the exception.
46	- **Never fix agent-generated code.** The benchmark measures autonomous output. Do not manually fix, edit, or patch workflow files in `workspaces/` or `results/*/generated-code/`.
47	- **`runner.py` observes and records, never intervenes** on agent code or errors.
48	- **Workspaces are throwaway.** Don't commit `workspaces/` contents.
49	- **`results/` is committed.** It contains archived metrics, generated code, and transcripts.
50	- **`CLAUDE.md` is only a pointer.** All instructions go in `AGENTS.md`. Never put substantive content in `CLAUDE.md` — it should only reference this file.
51	
52	## Architecture
53	
54	### Key files
55	
56	- `models.py` — single source of truth for model IDs and token pricing. Update here when Anthropic changes prices.
57	- `runner.py` — benchmark harness. Runs agents via `claude -p`, collects metrics, pushes results. Imports from `models.py` and `generate_results.py`.
58	- `generate_results.py` — generates `results.md` reports and updates `README.md`. Can run standalone: `python3 generate_results.py --all`.
59	- `test_quality.py` — test quality evaluation. Structural metrics (always) + LLM-as-judge (with `--llm-judge --provider <name>`). Imported by `generate_results.py` for the "Test Quality Evaluation" section.
60	- `llm_providers.py` — pluggable LLM provider abstraction for evaluation tasks (see "Adding LLM providers" below).
61	- `benchmark-instructions-v*.md` — per-version specs given to agents during runs.
62	- `hooks/syntax-check.py` — PostToolUse hook for syntax/lint checking.
63	- `Dockerfile.act` — custom act container image with pwsh + Pester pre-installed. Build with `docker build -t act-ubuntu-pwsh:latest -f Dockerfile.act .`. Runner.py auto-detects it and injects `.actrc` into workspaces.
64	- `skills/` — agent skills following [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io/specification) spec.
65	
66	### Adding new trap detectors
67	
68	See the docstring on `_detect_traps()` in `generate_results.py`. Each trap needs:
69	a kebab-case name, detection logic over bash_cmds/console/metrics, a time estimate,
70	and an entry in `trap_applicable_mode` if mode-specific.
71	
72	### LLM vs structural discrepancy checks
73	
74	After every report generation (`python3 generate_results.py --all`), check the
75	"LLM vs Structural Discrepancies" section in each `results.md`. Discrepancies
76	are auto-classified by `_find_discrepancies()` in `generate_results.py`:
77	
78	- **counter-gap**: structural metrics are implausibly low (e.g. 0 tests or 0
79	  assertions while LLM scores high). This means `test_quality.py` is missing a
80	  test pattern. **Fix the counter** — read the generated test files to identify
81	  the pattern the counter doesn't recognize, add it to the appropriate
82	  `_count_*()` function and the detection/classification patterns, add unit
83	  tests, and regenerate. Counter-gap discrepancies should not persist across PRs.
84	- **qualitative**: structural metrics look reasonable but the LLM disagrees on
85	  quality. The report includes the LLM's justification (from the `summary`
86	  field in `test-quality-llm.json`). These are expected and acceptable — review
87	  the justification to confirm it's coherent, then leave them.
88	
89	If a **new counter-gap** appears after changing `test_quality.py`, it's a
90	regression. Fix it before merging.
91	
92	### Updating model pricing

94	Edit `models.py`. Check https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models and

98	
99	After changing `generate_results.py`, run:
100	```bash
101	python3 generate_results.py --all
102	```
103	This regenerates `results.md` for every run directory and updates `README.md`.
104	
105	### Adding LLM providers
106	
107	The LLM-as-judge evaluation in `test_quality.py` uses a pluggable provider
108	system defined in `llm_providers.py`. The benchmark runner (`runner.py`) is
109	inherently tied to the Claude Code CLI (it tests CLI-specific features), but
110	the evaluation layer is provider-agnostic.
111

# Agent Instructions

All agent-facing instructions live in this file. `CLAUDE.md` contains only a
reference here. If you are a Claude Code agent, you have already loaded this
via `CLAUDE.md`. Other agents: read this file directly.

## Build and test

```bash
# Run unit tests (REQUIRED before every PR)
pip install pytest  # if not already installed
python3 -m pytest tests/ -v

# Validate imports
python3 -c "from runner import main"
python3 -c "from generate_results import generate_results_md"
python3 -c "from test_quality import compute_structural_metrics"
python3 -c "from llm_providers import get_provider"

# Regenerate all reports
python3 generate_results.py --all

# Run a benchmark (v4, all tasks/modes/models)
python3 runner.py --tasks 11,12,13,15,16,17,18 --modes default,powershell,bash,typescript-bun --models opus,sonnet

# Evaluate test quality (structural metrics only)
python3 test_quality.py results/2026-04-09_152435

# Evaluate test quality with LLM-as-judge (requires a provider)
python3 test_quality.py --llm-judge --provider claude-cli results/2026-04-09_152435

# Build custom act container (optional, eliminates pwsh install overhead)
docker build -t act-ubuntu-pwsh:latest -f Dockerfile.act .
```

## Code style

- Python 3.12+. No type stubs or mypy. Use type hints where they aid readability.
- Dollar amounts in results.md: round to nearest penny (`.2f`).
- Durations in results.md: always in minutes with 1 decimal (`{seconds/60:.1f}min`).
- No emojis in code or docs unless the user asks.

## Repository rules

- **No agent-generated `.github/workflows/` at repo root.** Agent workflows only exist inside workspaces under `workspaces/`. The repo's own CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) is the exception.
- **Never fix agent-generated code.** The benchmark measures autonomous output. Do not manually fix, edit, or patch workflow files in `workspaces/` or `results/*/generated-code/`.
- **`runner.py` observes and records, never intervenes** on agent code or errors.
- **Workspaces are throwaway.** Don't commit `workspaces/` contents.
- **`results/` is committed.** It contains archived metrics, generated code, and transcripts.
- **`CLAUDE.md` is only a pointer.** All instructions go in `AGENTS.md`. Never put substantive content in `CLAUDE.md` — it should only reference this file.

## Architecture

### Key files

- `models.py` — single source of truth for model IDs and token pricing. Update here when Anthropic changes prices.
- `runner.py` — benchmark harness. Runs agents via `claude -p`, collects metrics, pushes results. Imports from `models.py` and `generate_results.py`.
- `generate_results.py` — generates `results.md` reports and updates `README.md`. Can run standalone: `python3 generate_results.py --all`.
- `test_quality.py` — test quality evaluation. Structural metrics (always) + LLM-as-judge (with `--llm-judge --provider <name>`). Imported by `generate_results.py` for the "Test Quality Evaluation" section.
- `llm_providers.py` — pluggable LLM provider abstraction for evaluation tasks (see "Adding LLM providers" below).
- `benchmark-instructions-v*.md` — per-version specs given to agents during runs.
- `hooks/syntax-check.py` — PostToolUse hook for syntax/lint checking.
- `Dockerfile.act` — custom act container image with pwsh + Pester pre-installed. Build with `docker build -t act-ubuntu-pwsh:latest -f Dockerfile.act .`. Runner.py auto-detects it and injects `.actrc` into workspaces.
- `skills/` — agent skills following [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io/specification) spec.

### Adding new trap detectors

See the docstring on `_detect_traps()` in `generate_results.py`. Each trap needs:
a kebab-case name, detection logic over bash_cmds/console/metrics, a time estimate,
and an entry in `trap_applicable_mode` if mode-specific.

### LLM vs structural discrepancy checks

After every report generation (`python3 generate_results.py --all`), check the
"LLM vs Structural Discrepancies" section in each `results.md`. Discrepancies
are auto-classified by `_find_discrepancies()` in `generate_results.py`:

- **counter-gap**: structural metrics are implausibly low (e.g. 0 tests or 0
  assertions while LLM scores high). This means `test_quality.py` is missing a
  test pattern. **Fix the counter** — read the generated test files to identify
  the pattern the counter doesn't recognize, add it to the appropriate
  `_count_*()` function and the detection/classification patterns, add unit
  tests, and regenerate. Counter-gap discrepancies should not persist across PRs.
- **qualitative**: structural metrics look reasonable but the LLM disagrees on
  quality. The report includes the LLM's justification (from the `summary`
  field in `test-quality-llm.json`). These are expected and acceptable — review
  the justification to confirm it's coherent, then leave them.

If a **new counter-gap** appears after changing `test_quality.py`, it's a
regression. Fix it before merging.

### Updating model pricing

Edit `models.py`. Check https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models and
https://www.anthropic.com/pricing. Then run `python3 generate_results.py --all`.

### Regenerating reports

After changing `generate_results.py`, run:
```bash
python3 generate_results.py --all
```
This regenerates `results.md` for every run directory and updates `README.md`.

### Adding LLM providers

The LLM-as-judge evaluation in `test_quality.py` uses a pluggable provider
system defined in `llm_providers.py`. The benchmark runner (`runner.py`) is
inherently tied to the Claude Code CLI (it tests CLI-specific features), but
the evaluation layer is provider-agnostic.

To add a new provider (e.g., Anthropic API, OpenAI, Codex CLI):

1. Open `llm_providers.py` and create a class inheriting from `LLMProvider`.
2. Implement `is_available()` — return True when the provider can be used.
3. Implement `judge(system_prompt, user_message, model)` — return
   `{"text": str, "cost_usd": float, "input_tokens": int, "output_tokens": int}`.
4. Register it in the `PROVIDERS` dict at the bottom of the file.
5. Use it: `python3 test_quality.py --llm-judge --provider your-provider`.

See the docstring in `llm_providers.py` for a complete example skeleton.

## Before every PR

1. **Run all unit tests and verify they pass:**
   ```bash
   python3 -m pytest tests/ -v
   ```
   All tests must pass. Do not create or update a PR with failing tests.
2. **If you added or changed code, add or update unit tests** in `tests/`.
   New functions need test coverage. Changed behavior needs updated assertions.
3. Run `python3 generate_results.py --all` and verify no errors.
4. **Check for counter-gap discrepancies** in the generated `results.md` files.
   If any "Probable counter gaps" appear, fix them in `test_quality.py` before
   merging (see "LLM vs structural discrepancy checks" above). Qualitative
   disagreements are expected — verify the LLM justification is coherent.
5. Verify all import paths work: `python3 -c "from runner import main"`.
6. Spot-check a few numbers in results.md against raw metrics.json.
7. If you changed architecture or findings, update this file (`AGENTS.md`).
8. If you added files or moved things, update the Files table in `README.md`.

## Current state (2026-04-13)

### v4 benchmark — complete, task 14 archived

64/64 runs finished (8 tasks x 4 modes x 2 models). Results in
`results/2026-04-09_152435/`. Zero failures, zero timeouts, zero
double-result bugs. Total cost $86.90, avg 8.6min/run.

v4 added trap-awareness guidance from v3 findings, `shell: pwsh` for
PowerShell mode, "limit to 3 act push" instruction, and a custom act
Docker image with pwsh/Pester pre-installed. This cut average run time
by 24% vs v3 (8.6min vs 11.4min).

Post-v4 analysis found Task 14 (Docker Image Tag Generator) redundant
with Task 16 (Environment Matrix Generator) across TQ scores, cost, and
duration profiles. Task 14 was archived — see `archived-tasks/`. Future
runs use 7 tasks (11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18) x 4 modes x 2 models = 56 runs.

### Key findings (v4)

- Opus is faster than Sonnet across all modes.
- Default mode always chooses Python.
- PowerShell/sonnet is the slowest and most expensive combo.
- TypeScript hooks have ~50% catch rate but are net negative for Opus
  (tsc --noEmit takes 12-21s per Write on large files) and net positive
  for Sonnet (smaller writes, 2-3s per check).
- PowerShell hooks catch almost nothing (0-4% rate) — net negative.
- v4 trap-awareness guidance eliminated the timeout and double-result
  bugs that occurred in v3.

### Earlier versions

- v3: `results/2026-04-08_192624/` — 64 runs, same tasks/modes/models as v4. Had 1 timeout, 3 double-result bugs. Avg 11.4min/run.
- v2: `results/2026-04-07_225702/` — 111/144 runs. 18 tasks, modes: default/powershell/powershell-strict/csharp-script. Superseded by v3.
- v1: `results/2026-04-02_163146/` — 144 runs, same as v2. Had permission-denial artifacts (88% of errors).
- See `design-and-planning-artifacts/` for historical analysis and planning docs.

<#
Test Results Aggregator
Parses and aggregates test results from JUnit XML and JSON formats,
identifies flaky tests, and generates GitHub Actions job summary.
#>

function Get-JunitXmlTestResults {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [string]$FilePath
    )

    if (-not (Test-Path $FilePath)) {
        return @{Tests = @(); TotalTime = 0; Summary = @{Passed = 0; Failed = 0; Skipped = 0}}
    }

    [xml]$xml = Get-Content $FilePath
    $results = @{Tests = @(); TotalTime = 0; Summary = @{Passed = 0; Failed = 0; Skipped = 0}}

    foreach ($testsuite in $xml.testsuites.testsuite) {
        $results.TotalTime += [double]$testsuite.time
        foreach ($testcase in $testsuite.testcase) {
            $test = @{
                Name = $testcase.name
                Class = $testcase.classname
                Duration = [double]$testcase.time
                Status = "passed"
            }

            if ($testcase.failure) {
                $test.Status = "failed"
                $test.Message = $testcase.failure.message
                $results.Summary.Failed++
            }
            elseif ($testcase.skipped) {
                $test.Status = "skipped"
                $results.Summary.Skipped++
            }
            else {
                $results.Summary.Passed++
            }

            $results.Tests += $test
        }
    }

    return $results
}

function Get-JsonTestResults {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [string]$FilePath
    )

    if (-not (Test-Path $FilePath)) {
        return @{Tests = @(); TotalTime = 0; Summary = @{Passed = 0; Failed = 0; Skipped = 0}}
    }

    $json = Get-Content $FilePath | ConvertFrom-Json
    $results = @{Tests = @(); TotalTime = $json.duration; Summary = @{Passed = 0; Failed = 0; Skipped = 0}}

    foreach ($test in $json.tests) {
        $testObj = @{
            Name = $test.name
            Duration = $test.duration
            Status = $test.status
        }

        if ($test.status -eq "passed") {
            $results.Summary.Passed++
        }
        elseif ($test.status -eq "failed") {
            $results.Summary.Failed++
            $testObj.Message = $test.message
        }
        elseif ($test.status -eq "skipped") {
            $results.Summary.Skipped++
        }

        $results.Tests += $testObj
    }

    return $results
}

function Aggregate-TestResults {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [object[]]$TestResults = @()
    )

    $aggregated = @{
        Passed = 0
        Failed = 0
        Skipped = 0
        Duration = 0
        AllTests = @()
    }

    foreach ($result in $TestResults) {
        if ($result.Summary) {
            $aggregated.Passed += $result.Summary.Passed
            $aggregated.Failed += $result.Summary.Failed
            $aggregated.Skipped += $result.Summary.Skipped
        }
        $aggregated.Duration += $result.TotalTime
        $aggregated.AllTests += @($result.Tests)
    }

    return $aggregated
}

function ConvertTo-MarkdownSummary {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [object]$AggregatedResults
    )

    $markdown = @"
# Test Results Summary

## Totals

| Metric | Count |
|--------|-------|
| **Passed** | $($AggregatedResults.Passed) |
| **Failed** | $($AggregatedResults.Failed) |
| **Skipped** | $($AggregatedResults.Skipped) |
| **Total Duration** | $([math]::Round($AggregatedResults.Duration, 2))s |

"@

    return $markdown
}

function Find-FlakyTests {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [object[]]$MultipleRuns
    )

    $testStatuses = @{}

    foreach ($run in $MultipleRuns) {
        foreach ($test in $run.Tests) {
            if (-not $testStatuses[$test.Name]) {
                $testStatuses[$test.Name] = @()
            }
            $testStatuses[$test.Name] += $test.Status
        }
    }

    $flaky = @()
    foreach ($testName in $testStatuses.Keys) {
        $statuses = $testStatuses[$testName]
        if (($statuses -contains "passed") -and ($statuses -contains "failed")) {
            $flaky += @{Name = $testName; Statuses = $statuses}
        }
    }

    return $flaky
}

function Invoke-TestResultsAggregator {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [string[]]$TestResultFiles = @(),
        [string]$OutputFormat = "markdown"
    )

    # Placeholder implementation
    Write-Host "Test Results Aggregator initialized"
}

    }

    $json = Get-Content $FilePath | ConvertFrom-Json
    $results = @{Tests = @(); TotalTime = $json.duration; Summary = @{Passed = 0; Failed = 0; Skipped = 0}}

    foreach ($test in $json.tests) {
        $testObj = @{
            Name = $test.name
            Duration = $test.duration
            Status = $test.status
        }

        if ($test.status -eq "passed") {
            $results.Summary.Passed++
        }
        elseif ($test.status -eq "failed") {
            $results.Summary.Failed++
            $testObj.Message = $test.message
        }
        elseif ($test.status -eq "skipped") {
            $results.Summary.Skipped++
        }

        $results.Tests += $testObj
    }

    return $results
}

function Aggregate-TestResults {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [object[]]$TestResults = @()
    )

    $aggregated = @{
        Passed = 0
        Failed = 0
        Skipped = 0
        Duration = 0
        AllTests = @()
    }

    foreach ($result in $TestResults) {
        if ($result.Summary) {
            $aggregated.Passed += $result.Summary.Passed
            $aggregated.Failed += $result.Summary.Failed
            $aggregated.Skipped += $result.Summary.Skipped
        }
        $aggregated.Duration += $result.TotalTime
        $aggregated.AllTests += @($result.Tests)
    }

    return $aggregated
}

function ConvertTo-MarkdownSummary {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [object]$AggregatedResults
    )

    $markdown = @"
# Test Results Summary

## Totals

| Metric | Count |
|--------|-------|
| **Passed** | $($AggregatedResults.Passed) |
| **Failed** | $($AggregatedResults.Failed) |
| **Skipped** | $($AggregatedResults.Skipped) |
| **Total Duration** | $([math]::Round($AggregatedResults.Duration, 2))s |

"@

    return $markdown
}

function Find-FlakyTests {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [object[]]$MultipleRuns
    )

    $testStatuses = @{}

    foreach ($run in $MultipleRuns) {
        foreach ($test in $run.Tests) {
            if (-not $testStatuses[$test.Name]) {
                $testStatuses[$test.Name] = @()
            }
            $testStatuses[$test.Name] += $test.Status
        }
    }

    $flaky = @()
    foreach ($testName in $testStatuses.Keys) {
        $statuses = $testStatuses[$testName]
        if (($statuses -contains "passed") -and ($statuses -contains "failed")) {
            $flaky += @{Name = $testName; Statuses = $statuses}
        }
    }

    return $flaky
}

function Invoke-TestResultsAggregator {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [string[]]$TestResultFiles = @(),
        [string]$OutputFormat = "markdown"
    )

    # Placeholder implementation
    Write-Host "Test Results Aggregator initialized"
}

name: Test Results Aggregator

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 0 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  actions: read

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Run Tests and Aggregate Results

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Run all tests in the repository
          $testResults = Invoke-Pester -Path "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1" -PassThru

          # Check if tests passed
          if ($testResults.FailedCount -gt 0) {
            Write-Host "::error::$($testResults.FailedCount) test(s) failed"
            exit 1
          }

          Write-Host "All $($testResults.PassedCount) tests passed successfully"

      - name: Test fixture parsing
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Source the aggregator script
          . ./test-results-aggregator.ps1

          # Test JUnit XML parsing
          $xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run1.xml"
          $xml2 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run2.xml"

          Write-Host "Run 1 - Passed: $($xml1.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($xml1.Summary.Failed), Skipped: $($xml1.Summary.Skipped)"
          Write-Host "Run 2 - Passed: $($xml2.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($xml2.Summary.Failed), Skipped: $($xml2.Summary.Skipped)"

          # Test JSON parsing
          $json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run1.json"
          $json2 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run2.json"

          Write-Host "JSON Run 1 - Passed: $($json1.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($json1.Summary.Failed)"
          Write-Host "JSON Run 2 - Passed: $($json2.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($json2.Summary.Failed)"

          # Identify flaky tests
          $flaky = Find-FlakyTests -MultipleRuns @($xml1, $xml2)
          if ($flaky) {
            Write-Host "Flaky tests detected:"
            foreach ($test in $flaky) {
              Write-Host "  - $($test.Name): $($test.Statuses -join ', ')"
            }
          }

      - name: Generate markdown summary
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Source the aggregator script
          . ./test-results-aggregator.ps1

          # Parse all test files
          $xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run1.xml"
          $xml2 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run2.xml"
          $json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run1.json"
          $json2 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run2.json"

          # Aggregate results
          $allResults = Aggregate-TestResults -TestResults @($xml1, $xml2, $json1, $json2)

          # Convert to markdown
          $markdown = ConvertTo-MarkdownSummary -AggregatedResults $allResults

          # Find flaky tests
          $flaky = Find-FlakyTests -MultipleRuns @($xml1, $xml2, $json1, $json2)

          # Append flaky tests section if any found
          if ($flaky) {
            $markdown += "`n## Flaky Tests`n`n"
            foreach ($test in $flaky) {
              $markdown += "- **$($test.Name)**: Inconsistent across runs`n"
            }
          }

          # Write to job summary
          $markdown | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY -Encoding utf8 -Append

          Write-Host "Summary written to GitHub job summary"
          Write-Host $markdown

  validate-workflow:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Validate Workflow Structure

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Verify script files exist
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $requiredFiles = @(
            "test-results-aggregator.ps1",
            "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1",
            "fixtures/junit-run1.xml",
            "fixtures/junit-run2.xml",
            "fixtures/results-run1.json",
            "fixtures/results-run2.json"
          )

          foreach ($file in $requiredFiles) {
            if (-not (Test-Path $file)) {
              Write-Host "::error::Required file missing: $file"
              exit 1
            }
            Write-Host "✓ $file exists"
          }

      - name: Validate YAML syntax
        shell: bash
        run: |
          # Check if actionlint is available
          if command -v actionlint &> /dev/null; then
            actionlint .github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml
          else
            echo "actionlint not found, skipping YAML validation"
          fi

content.text: Now let me create the test harness that will run the workflow through `act`:

input.content:
<#
Test harness for running the GitHub Actions workflow locally with act.
Tests the complete end-to-end pipeline using Docker and act.
#>

param(
    [string]$OutputFile = "act-result.txt"
)

function Write-TestLog {
    param([string]$Message)
    $timestamp = Get-Date -Format "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
    $logEntry = "[$timestamp] $Message"
    Write-Host $logEntry
    Add-Content -Path $OutputFile -Value $logEntry
}

function Test-WorkflowStructure {
    Write-TestLog "=== WORKFLOW STRUCTURE TESTS ==="

    # Test 1: Check that workflow file exists
    Write-TestLog "Test 1: Checking workflow file exists..."
    if (-not (Test-Path "./.github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml")) {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow file not found"
        return $false
    }
    Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow file exists"

    # Test 2: Verify required script files exist
    Write-TestLog "Test 2: Checking required script files..."
    $requiredFiles = @(
        "test-results-aggregator.ps1",
        "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1",
        "fixtures/junit-run1.xml",
        "fixtures/junit-run2.xml",
        "fixtures/results-run1.json",
        "fixtures/results-run2.json"
    )

    foreach ($file in $requiredFiles) {
        if (-not (Test-Path $file)) {
            Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Required file missing: $file"
            return $false
        }
        Write-TestLog "  ✓ $file"
    }

    # Test 3: Validate workflow YAML with actionlint
    Write-TestLog "Test 3: Validating workflow YAML..."
    $actionlintOutput = & actionlint ".github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml" 2>&1
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ actionlint validation passed"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: actionlint validation failed"
        Write-TestLog $actionlintOutput
        return $false
    }

    # Test 4: Check workflow has expected jobs
    Write-TestLog "Test 4: Verifying workflow job structure..."
    [xml]$workflowYaml = (Get-Content ".github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml" -Raw) -replace 'on:', 'on_trigger:'
    $workflow = Get-Content ".github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml" -Raw

    if ($workflow -match "jobs:") {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow has jobs section"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow missing jobs section"
        return $false
    }

    if ($workflow -match "test:" -and $workflow -match "validate-workflow:") {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow has test and validate-workflow jobs"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow missing required jobs"
        return $false
    }

    Write-TestLog "=== ALL WORKFLOW STRUCTURE TESTS PASSED ==="
    return $true
}

function Test-WorkflowExecution {
    Write-TestLog "`n=== WORKFLOW EXECUTION TESTS ==="

    # Test 1: Run workflow with act on push event
    Write-TestLog "Test 1: Executing workflow with 'act push' (this may take 1-2 minutes)..."

    $tempDir = New-Item -ItemType Directory -Name "act-test-temp" -Force
    Push-Location $tempDir.FullName

    try {
        # Copy necessary files to temp directory
        Copy-Item -Path "../test-results-aggregator.ps1" -Destination "./"
        Copy-Item -Path "../test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1" -Destination "./"
        Copy-Item -Path "../.github" -Destination "./" -Recurse -Force
        Copy-Item -Path "../fixtures" -Destination "./" -Recurse -Force
        Copy-Item -Path "../.actrc" -Destination "./" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

        # Initialize git repo for act
        & git init --initial-branch=main | Out-Null
        & git config user.email "test@example.com" | Out-Null
        & git config user.name "Test User" | Out-Null
        & git add . | Out-Null
        & git commit -m "Initial commit" | Out-Null

        # Run the workflow
        $actOutput = & act push --rm 2>&1
        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
            Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow execution failed with exit code $LASTEXITCODE"
            Write-TestLog "Act output:"
            Write-TestLog $actOutput
            return $false
        }

        Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow executed successfully"
        Write-TestLog "Act output summary:"
        $actOutput | ForEach-Object { Write-TestLog "  $_" }

        # Test 2: Verify job succeeded
        Write-TestLog "Test 2: Verifying job completion status..."
        $output = $actOutput | Out-String

        if ($output -match "Job succeeded") {
            Write-TestLog "✓ At least one job succeeded"
        }
        else {
            Write-TestLog "⚠ Warning: Could not verify job success status in output"
        }

        # Test 3: Check for test execution messages
        Write-TestLog "Test 3: Verifying test execution..."
        if ($output -match "tests passed" -or $output -match "passed successfully") {
            Write-TestLog "✓ Tests execution detected in output"
        }
        else {
            Write-TestLog "⚠ Warning: Could not verify test execution in output"
        }

    }
    finally {
        Pop-Location
        Remove-Item -Path $tempDir.FullName -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    }

    Write-TestLog "=== WORKFLOW EXECUTION TEST COMPLETED ==="
    return $true
}

function Test-LocalExecution {
    Write-TestLog "`n=== LOCAL EXECUTION TESTS ==="

    # Test 1: Run Pester tests locally
    Write-TestLog "Test 1: Running Pester tests locally..."
    $testResults = & pwsh -Command "Invoke-Pester -Path './test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1' -PassThru"

    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Local Pester tests passed"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Local Pester tests failed"
        return $false
    }

    # Test 2: Test fixture parsing
    Write-TestLog "Test 2: Testing fixture parsing..."
    $parseTest = & pwsh -Command @"
. ./test-results-aggregator.ps1
\$xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath './fixtures/junit-run1.xml'
\$json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath './fixtures/results-run1.json'
if (\$xml1.Summary.Passed -eq 3 -and \$json1.Summary.Passed -eq 3) {
    exit 0
} else {
    exit 1
}
"@

    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Fixture parsing works correctly"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Fixture parsing test failed"
        return $false
    }

    Write-TestLog "=== ALL LOCAL EXECUTION TESTS PASSED ==="
    return $true
}

# Main execution
Clear-Content -Path $OutputFile -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Write-TestLog "Test harness starting..."
Write-TestLog "Output file: $OutputFile"

$results = @{
    Structure = Test-WorkflowStructure
    Local = Test-LocalExecution
    Workflow = Test-WorkflowExecution
}

Write-TestLog "`n=== TEST SUMMARY ==="
$passed = 0
$failed = 0

foreach ($testName in $results.Keys) {
    if ($results[$testName]) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ $testName tests: PASSED"
        $passed++
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ $testName tests: FAILED"
        $failed++
    }
}

Write-TestLog "Total: $passed passed, $failed failed"

if ($failed -eq 0) {
    Write-TestLog "ALL TESTS PASSED ✓"
    exit 0
}
else {
    Write-TestLog "SOME TESTS FAILED ❌"
    exit 1
}

        "test-results-aggregator.ps1",
        "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1",
        "fixtures/junit-run1.xml",
        "fixtures/junit-run2.xml",
        "fixtures/results-run1.json",
        "fixtures/results-run2.json"
    )

    foreach ($file in $requiredFiles) {
        if (-not (Test-Path $file)) {
            Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Required file missing: $file"
            return $false
        }
        Write-TestLog "  ✓ $file"
    }

    # Test 3: Validate workflow YAML with actionlint
    Write-TestLog "Test 3: Validating workflow YAML..."
    $actionlintOutput = & actionlint ".github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml" 2>&1
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ actionlint validation passed"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: actionlint validation failed"
        Write-TestLog $actionlintOutput
        return $false
    }

    # Test 4: Check workflow has expected jobs
    Write-TestLog "Test 4: Verifying workflow job structure..."
    [xml]$workflowYaml = (Get-Content ".github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml" -Raw) -replace 'on:', 'on_trigger:'
    $workflow = Get-Content ".github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml" -Raw

    if ($workflow -match "jobs:") {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow has jobs section"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow missing jobs section"
        return $false
    }

    if ($workflow -match "test:" -and $workflow -match "validate-workflow:") {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow has test and validate-workflow jobs"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow missing required jobs"
        return $false
    }

    Write-TestLog "=== ALL WORKFLOW STRUCTURE TESTS PASSED ==="
    return $true
}

function Test-WorkflowExecution {
    Write-TestLog "`n=== WORKFLOW EXECUTION TESTS ==="

    # Test 1: Run workflow with act on push event
    Write-TestLog "Test 1: Executing workflow with 'act push' (this may take 1-2 minutes)..."

    $tempDir = New-Item -ItemType Directory -Name "act-test-temp" -Force
    Push-Location $tempDir.FullName

    try {
        # Copy necessary files to temp directory
        Copy-Item -Path "../test-results-aggregator.ps1" -Destination "./"
        Copy-Item -Path "../test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1" -Destination "./"
        Copy-Item -Path "../.github" -Destination "./" -Recurse -Force
        Copy-Item -Path "../fixtures" -Destination "./" -Recurse -Force
        Copy-Item -Path "../.actrc" -Destination "./" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

        # Initialize git repo for act
        & git init --initial-branch=main | Out-Null
        & git config user.email "test@example.com" | Out-Null
        & git config user.name "Test User" | Out-Null
        & git add . | Out-Null
        & git commit -m "Initial commit" | Out-Null

        # Run the workflow
        $actOutput = & act push --rm 2>&1
        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
            Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow execution failed with exit code $LASTEXITCODE"
            Write-TestLog "Act output:"
            Write-TestLog $actOutput
            return $false
        }

        Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow executed successfully"
        Write-TestLog "Act output summary:"
        $actOutput | ForEach-Object { Write-TestLog "  $_" }

        # Test 2: Verify job succeeded
        Write-TestLog "Test 2: Verifying job completion status..."
        $output = $actOutput | Out-String

        if ($output -match "Job succeeded") {
            Write-TestLog "✓ At least one job succeeded"
        }
        else {
            Write-TestLog "⚠ Warning: Could not verify job success status in output"
        }

        # Test 3: Check for test execution messages
        Write-TestLog "Test 3: Verifying test execution..."
        if ($output -match "tests passed" -or $output -match "passed successfully") {
            Write-TestLog "✓ Tests execution detected in output"
        }
        else {
            Write-TestLog "⚠ Warning: Could not verify test execution in output"
        }

    }
    finally {
        Pop-Location
        Remove-Item -Path $tempDir.FullName -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    }

    Write-TestLog "=== WORKFLOW EXECUTION TEST COMPLETED ==="
    return $true
}

function Test-LocalExecution {
    Write-TestLog "`n=== LOCAL EXECUTION TESTS ==="

    # Test 1: Run Pester tests locally
    Write-TestLog "Test 1: Running Pester tests locally..."
    $testResults = & pwsh -Command "Invoke-Pester -Path './test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1' -PassThru"

    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Local Pester tests passed"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Local Pester tests failed"
        return $false
    }

    # Test 2: Test fixture parsing
    Write-TestLog "Test 2: Testing fixture parsing..."
    $parseTest = & pwsh -Command @"
. ./test-results-aggregator.ps1
\$xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath './fixtures/junit-run1.xml'
\$json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath './fixtures/results-run1.json'
if (\$xml1.Summary.Passed -eq 3 -and \$json1.Summary.Passed -eq 3) {
    exit 0
} else {
    exit 1
}
"@

    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Fixture parsing works correctly"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Fixture parsing test failed"
        return $false
    }

    Write-TestLog "=== ALL LOCAL EXECUTION TESTS PASSED ==="
    return $true
}

# Main execution
Clear-Content -Path $OutputFile -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Write-TestLog "Test harness starting..."
Write-TestLog "Output file: $OutputFile"

$results = @{
    Structure = Test-WorkflowStructure
    Local = Test-LocalExecution
    Workflow = Test-WorkflowExecution
}

Write-TestLog "`n=== TEST SUMMARY ==="
$passed = 0
$failed = 0

foreach ($testName in $results.Keys) {
    if ($results[$testName]) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ $testName tests: PASSED"
        $passed++
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ $testName tests: FAILED"
        $failed++
    }
}

Write-TestLog "Total: $passed passed, $failed failed"

if ($failed -eq 0) {
    Write-TestLog "ALL TESTS PASSED ✓"
    exit 0
}
else {
    Write-TestLog "SOME TESTS FAILED ❌"
    exit 1
}

<#
Test harness for running the GitHub Actions workflow locally with act.
Tests the complete end-to-end pipeline using Docker and act.
#>

param(
    [string]$OutputFile = "act-result.txt"
)

function Write-TestLog {
    param([string]$Message)
    $timestamp = Get-Date -Format "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
    $logEntry = "[$timestamp] $Message"
    Write-Host $logEntry
    Add-Content -Path $OutputFile -Value $logEntry
}

function Test-WorkflowStructure {
    Write-TestLog "=== WORKFLOW STRUCTURE TESTS ==="

    # Test 1: Check that workflow file exists
    Write-TestLog "Test 1: Checking workflow file exists..."
    if (-not (Test-Path "./.github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml")) {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow file not found"
        return $false
    }
    Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow file exists"

    # Test 2: Verify required script files exist
    Write-TestLog "Test 2: Checking required script files..."
    $requiredFiles = @(
        "test-results-aggregator.ps1",
        "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1",
        "fixtures/junit-run1.xml",
        "fixtures/junit-run2.xml",
        "fixtures/results-run1.json",
        "fixtures/results-run2.json"
    )

    foreach ($file in $requiredFiles) {
        if (-not (Test-Path $file)) {
            Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Required file missing: $file"
            return $false
        }
        Write-TestLog "  ✓ $file"
    }

    # Test 3: Validate workflow YAML with actionlint
    Write-TestLog "Test 3: Validating workflow YAML..."
    $actionlintOutput = & actionlint ".github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml" 2>&1
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ actionlint validation passed"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: actionlint validation failed"
        Write-TestLog $actionlintOutput
        return $false
    }

    # Test 4: Check workflow has expected jobs
    Write-TestLog "Test 4: Verifying workflow job structure..."
    [xml]$workflowYaml = (Get-Content ".github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml" -Raw) -replace 'on:', 'on_trigger:'
    $workflow = Get-Content ".github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml" -Raw

    if ($workflow -match "jobs:") {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow has jobs section"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow missing jobs section"
        return $false
    }

    if ($workflow -match "test:" -and $workflow -match "validate-workflow:") {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow has test and validate-workflow jobs"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow missing required jobs"
        return $false
    }

    Write-TestLog "=== ALL WORKFLOW STRUCTURE TESTS PASSED ==="
    return $true
}

function Test-WorkflowExecution {
    Write-TestLog "`n=== WORKFLOW EXECUTION TESTS ==="

    # Test 1: Run workflow with act on push event
    Write-TestLog "Test 1: Executing workflow with 'act push' (this may take 1-2 minutes)..."

    $tempDir = New-Item -ItemType Directory -Name "act-test-temp" -Force
    Push-Location $tempDir.FullName

    try {
        # Copy necessary files to temp directory
        Copy-Item -Path "../test-results-aggregator.ps1" -Destination "./"
        Copy-Item -Path "../test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1" -Destination "./"
        Copy-Item -Path "../.github" -Destination "./" -Recurse -Force
        Copy-Item -Path "../fixtures" -Destination "./" -Recurse -Force
        Copy-Item -Path "../.actrc" -Destination "./" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

        # Initialize git repo for act
        & git init --initial-branch=main | Out-Null
        & git config user.email "test@example.com" | Out-Null
        & git config user.name "Test User" | Out-Null
        & git add . | Out-Null
        & git commit -m "Initial commit" | Out-Null

        # Run the workflow
        $actOutput = & act push --rm 2>&1
        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
            Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow execution failed with exit code $LASTEXITCODE"
            Write-TestLog "Act output:"
            Write-TestLog $actOutput
            return $false
        }

        Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow executed successfully"
        Write-TestLog "Act output summary:"
        $actOutput | ForEach-Object { Write-TestLog "  $_" }

        # Test 2: Verify job succeeded
        Write-TestLog "Test 2: Verifying job completion status..."
        $output = $actOutput | Out-String

        if ($output -match "Job succeeded") {
            Write-TestLog "✓ At least one job succeeded"
        }
        else {
            Write-TestLog "⚠ Warning: Could not verify job success status in output"
        }

        # Test 3: Check for test execution messages
        Write-TestLog "Test 3: Verifying test execution..."
        if ($output -match "tests passed" -or $output -match "passed successfully") {
            Write-TestLog "✓ Tests execution detected in output"
        }
        else {
            Write-TestLog "⚠ Warning: Could not verify test execution in output"
        }

    }
    finally {
        Pop-Location
        Remove-Item -Path $tempDir.FullName -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    }

    Write-TestLog "=== WORKFLOW EXECUTION TEST COMPLETED ==="
    return $true
}

function Test-LocalExecution {
    Write-TestLog "`n=== LOCAL EXECUTION TESTS ==="

    # Test 1: Run Pester tests locally
    Write-TestLog "Test 1: Running Pester tests locally..."
    $testResults = & pwsh -Command "Invoke-Pester -Path './test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1' -PassThru"

    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Local Pester tests passed"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Local Pester tests failed"
        return $false
    }

    # Test 2: Test fixture parsing
    Write-TestLog "Test 2: Testing fixture parsing..."
    $parseTest = & pwsh -Command @"
. ./test-results-aggregator.ps1
\$xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath './fixtures/junit-run1.xml'
\$json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath './fixtures/results-run1.json'
if (\$xml1.Summary.Passed -eq 3 -and \$json1.Summary.Passed -eq 3) {
    exit 0
} else {
    exit 1
}
"@

    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Fixture parsing works correctly"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Fixture parsing test failed"
        return $false
    }

    Write-TestLog "=== ALL LOCAL EXECUTION TESTS PASSED ==="
    return $true
}

# Main execution
Clear-Content -Path $OutputFile -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Write-TestLog "Test harness starting..."
Write-TestLog "Output file: $OutputFile"

$results = @{
    Structure = Test-WorkflowStructure
    Local = Test-LocalExecution
    Workflow = Test-WorkflowExecution
}

Write-TestLog "`n=== TEST SUMMARY ==="
$passed = 0
$failed = 0

foreach ($testName in $results.Keys) {
    if ($results[$testName]) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ $testName tests: PASSED"
        $passed++
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ $testName tests: FAILED"
        $failed++
    }
}

Write-TestLog "Total: $passed passed, $failed failed"

if ($failed -eq 0) {
    Write-TestLog "ALL TESTS PASSED ✓"
    exit 0
}
else {
    Write-TestLog "SOME TESTS FAILED ❌"
    exit 1
}

There are two issues:

Let me fix the fixture parsing test issue first in the test harness, and then use the standard ubuntu-latest image instead of the custom one.

tool_use_result.originalFile:
<#
Test harness for running the GitHub Actions workflow locally with act.
Tests the complete end-to-end pipeline using Docker and act.
#>

param(
    [string]$OutputFile = "act-result.txt"
)

function Write-TestLog {
    param([string]$Message)
    $timestamp = Get-Date -Format "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
    $logEntry = "[$timestamp] $Message"
    Write-Host $logEntry
    Add-Content -Path $OutputFile -Value $logEntry
}

function Test-WorkflowStructure {
    Write-TestLog "=== WORKFLOW STRUCTURE TESTS ==="

    # Test 1: Check that workflow file exists
    Write-TestLog "Test 1: Checking workflow file exists..."
    if (-not (Test-Path "./.github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml")) {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow file not found"
        return $false
    }
    Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow file exists"

    # Test 2: Verify required script files exist
    Write-TestLog "Test 2: Checking required script files..."
    $requiredFiles = @(
        "test-results-aggregator.ps1",
        "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1",
        "fixtures/junit-run1.xml",
        "fixtures/junit-run2.xml",
        "fixtures/results-run1.json",
        "fixtures/results-run2.json"
    )

    foreach ($file in $requiredFiles) {
        if (-not (Test-Path $file)) {
            Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Required file missing: $file"
            return $false
        }
        Write-TestLog "  ✓ $file"
    }

    # Test 3: Validate workflow YAML with actionlint
    Write-TestLog "Test 3: Validating workflow YAML..."
    $actionlintOutput = & actionlint ".github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml" 2>&1
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ actionlint validation passed"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: actionlint validation failed"
        Write-TestLog $actionlintOutput
        return $false
    }

    # Test 4: Check workflow has expected jobs
    Write-TestLog "Test 4: Verifying workflow job structure..."
    $workflow = Get-Content ".github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml" -Raw

    if ($workflow -match "jobs:") {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow has jobs section"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow missing jobs section"
        return $false
    }

    if ($workflow -match "test:" -and $workflow -match "validate-workflow:") {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow has test and validate-workflow jobs"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow missing required jobs"
        return $false
    }

    Write-TestLog "=== ALL WORKFLOW STRUCTURE TESTS PASSED ==="
    return $true
}

function Test-WorkflowExecution {
    Write-TestLog "`n=== WORKFLOW EXECUTION TESTS ==="

    # Test 1: Run workflow with act on push event
    Write-TestLog "Test 1: Executing workflow with 'act push' (this may take 1-2 minutes)..."

    $tempDir = New-Item -ItemType Directory -Name "act-test-temp" -Force
    Push-Location $tempDir.FullName

    try {
        # Copy necessary files to temp directory
        Copy-Item -Path "../test-results-aggregator.ps1" -Destination "./"
        Copy-Item -Path "../test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1" -Destination "./"
        Copy-Item -Path "../.github" -Destination "./" -Recurse -Force
        Copy-Item -Path "../fixtures" -Destination "./" -Recurse -Force
        Copy-Item -Path "../.actrc" -Destination "./" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

        # Initialize git repo for act
        & git init --initial-branch=main | Out-Null
        & git config user.email "test@example.com" | Out-Null
        & git config user.name "Test User" | Out-Null
        & git add . | Out-Null
        & git commit -m "Initial commit" | Out-Null

        # Run the workflow
        $actOutput = & act push --rm 2>&1
        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
            Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Workflow execution failed with exit code $LASTEXITCODE"
            Write-TestLog "Act output:"
            Write-TestLog $actOutput
            return $false
        }

        Write-TestLog "✓ Workflow executed successfully"
        Write-TestLog "Act output summary:"
        $actOutput | ForEach-Object { Write-TestLog "  $_" }

        # Test 2: Verify job succeeded
        Write-TestLog "Test 2: Verifying job completion status..."
        $output = $actOutput | Out-String

        if ($output -match "Job succeeded") {
            Write-TestLog "✓ At least one job succeeded"
        }
        else {
            Write-TestLog "⚠ Warning: Could not verify job success status in output"
        }

        # Test 3: Check for test execution messages
        Write-TestLog "Test 3: Verifying test execution..."
        if ($output -match "tests passed" -or $output -match "passed successfully") {
            Write-TestLog "✓ Tests execution detected in output"
        }
        else {
            Write-TestLog "⚠ Warning: Could not verify test execution in output"
        }

    }
    finally {
        Pop-Location
        Remove-Item -Path $tempDir.FullName -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    }

    Write-TestLog "=== WORKFLOW EXECUTION TEST COMPLETED ==="
    return $true
}

function Test-LocalExecution {
    Write-TestLog "`n=== LOCAL EXECUTION TESTS ==="

    # Test 1: Run Pester tests locally
    Write-TestLog "Test 1: Running Pester tests locally..."
    $testResults = & pwsh -Command "Invoke-Pester -Path './test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1' -PassThru"

    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Local Pester tests passed"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Local Pester tests failed"
        return $false
    }

    # Test 2: Test fixture parsing
    Write-TestLog "Test 2: Testing fixture parsing..."
    $parseTest = & pwsh -Command @"
. ./test-results-aggregator.ps1
\$xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath './fixtures/junit-run1.xml'
\$json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath './fixtures/results-run1.json'
if (\$xml1.Summary.Passed -eq 3 -and \$json1.Summary.Passed -eq 3) {
    exit 0
} else {
    exit 1
}
"@

    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ Fixture parsing works correctly"
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ FAILED: Fixture parsing test failed"
        return $false
    }

    Write-TestLog "=== ALL LOCAL EXECUTION TESTS PASSED ==="
    return $true
}

# Main execution
Clear-Content -Path $OutputFile -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Write-TestLog "Test harness starting..."
Write-TestLog "Output file: $OutputFile"

$results = @{
    Structure = Test-WorkflowStructure
    Local = Test-LocalExecution
    Workflow = Test-WorkflowExecution
}

Write-TestLog "`n=== TEST SUMMARY ==="
$passed = 0
$failed = 0

foreach ($testName in $results.Keys) {
    if ($results[$testName]) {
        Write-TestLog "✓ $testName tests: PASSED"
        $passed++
    }
    else {
        Write-TestLog "❌ $testName tests: FAILED"
        $failed++
    }
}

Write-TestLog "Total: $passed passed, $failed failed"

if ($failed -eq 0) {
    Write-TestLog "ALL TESTS PASSED ✓"
    exit 0
}
else {
    Write-TestLog "SOME TESTS FAILED ❌"
    exit 1
}

tool_use_result.originalFile:
name: Test Results Aggregator

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 0 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  actions: read

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Run Tests and Aggregate Results

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Run all tests in the repository
          $testResults = Invoke-Pester -Path "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1" -PassThru

          # Check if tests passed
          if ($testResults.FailedCount -gt 0) {
            Write-Host "::error::$($testResults.FailedCount) test(s) failed"
            exit 1
          }

          Write-Host "All $($testResults.PassedCount) tests passed successfully"

      - name: Test fixture parsing
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Source the aggregator script
          . ./test-results-aggregator.ps1

          # Test JUnit XML parsing
          $xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run1.xml"
          $xml2 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run2.xml"

          Write-Host "Run 1 - Passed: $($xml1.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($xml1.Summary.Failed), Skipped: $($xml1.Summary.Skipped)"
          Write-Host "Run 2 - Passed: $($xml2.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($xml2.Summary.Failed), Skipped: $($xml2.Summary.Skipped)"

          # Test JSON parsing
          $json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run1.json"
          $json2 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run2.json"

          Write-Host "JSON Run 1 - Passed: $($json1.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($json1.Summary.Failed)"
          Write-Host "JSON Run 2 - Passed: $($json2.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($json2.Summary.Failed)"

          # Identify flaky tests
          $flaky = Find-FlakyTests -MultipleRuns @($xml1, $xml2)
          if ($flaky) {
            Write-Host "Flaky tests detected:"
            foreach ($test in $flaky) {
              Write-Host "  - $($test.Name): $($test.Statuses -join ', ')"
            }
          }

      - name: Generate markdown summary
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Source the aggregator script
          . ./test-results-aggregator.ps1

          # Parse all test files
          $xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run1.xml"
          $xml2 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run2.xml"
          $json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run1.json"
          $json2 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run2.json"

          # Aggregate results
          $allResults = Aggregate-TestResults -TestResults @($xml1, $xml2, $json1, $json2)

          # Convert to markdown
          $markdown = ConvertTo-MarkdownSummary -AggregatedResults $allResults

          # Find flaky tests
          $flaky = Find-FlakyTests -MultipleRuns @($xml1, $xml2, $json1, $json2)

          # Append flaky tests section if any found
          if ($flaky) {
            $markdown += "`n## Flaky Tests`n`n"
            foreach ($test in $flaky) {
              $markdown += "- **$($test.Name)**: Inconsistent across runs`n"
            }
          }

          # Write to job summary
          $markdown | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY -Encoding utf8 -Append

          Write-Host "Summary written to GitHub job summary"
          Write-Host $markdown

  validate-workflow:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Validate Workflow Structure

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Verify script files exist
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $requiredFiles = @(
            "test-results-aggregator.ps1",
            "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1",
            "fixtures/junit-run1.xml",
            "fixtures/junit-run2.xml",
            "fixtures/results-run1.json",
            "fixtures/results-run2.json"
          )

          foreach ($file in $requiredFiles) {
            if (-not (Test-Path $file)) {
              Write-Host "::error::Required file missing: $file"
              exit 1
            }
            Write-Host "✓ $file exists"
          }

      - name: Validate YAML syntax
        shell: bash
        run: |
          # Check if actionlint is available
          if command -v actionlint &> /dev/null; then
            actionlint .github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml
          else
            echo "actionlint not found, skipping YAML validation"
          fi

So we should have: 3 passed, 1 failed, 1 skipped. But my output shows 4 passed. Let me check my parsing logic again. Actually, looking at it, I see I may have missed that I'm not checking for failure before I add it. Let me trace through the logic:

1. For each testcase, I initialize status to "passed"
2. Then I check if testcase.failure exists - if it does, set status to "failed" and increment Failed counter
3. Else if testcase.skipped exists - set status to "skipped" and increment Skipped counter
4. Else increment Passed counter

So for Test2 with a failure element, the logic should:
- Initialize status to "passed"
- Check if testcase.failure exists - YES, so set status to "failed" and increment Failed counter

But the output shows 4 passed. Let me look more carefully...

Oh wait, I think the issue is that I'm not incrementing Passed for failed tests. Let me look at the code again:

```powershell
if ($testcase.failure) {
    $test.Status = "failed"
    $test.Message = $testcase.failure.message

}
elseif ($testcase.skipped) {

}
else {
    $results.Summary.Passed++
}
```

This looks correct. If it has a failure, we increment Failed (not Passed). If it has skipped, we increment Skipped. Otherwise, we increment Passed.


Walking through each test: Test1 hits the else branch for Passed++, Test2 should hit the if branch for Failed++, Test3 hits else for Passed++, Test4 hits elseif for Skipped++, and FlakyTest hits else for Passed++. That gives Passed=3, Failed=1, Skipped=1, but I'm seeing Passed=4 instead. The issue might be with how PowerShell is evaluating the XML element existence check—$testcase.failure might not be detecting the element correctly.

<#
Test Results Aggregator
Parses and aggregates test results from JUnit XML and JSON formats,
identifies flaky tests, and generates GitHub Actions job summary.
#>

function Get-JunitXmlTestResults {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [string]$FilePath
    )

    if (-not (Test-Path $FilePath)) {
        return @{Tests = @(); TotalTime = 0; Summary = @{Passed = 0; Failed = 0; Skipped = 0}}
    }

    [xml]$xml = Get-Content $FilePath
    $results = @{Tests = @(); TotalTime = 0; Summary = @{Passed = 0; Failed = 0; Skipped = 0}}

    foreach ($testsuite in $xml.testsuites.testsuite) {
        $results.TotalTime += [double]$testsuite.time
        foreach ($testcase in $testsuite.testcase) {
            $test = @{
                Name = $testcase.name
                Class = $testcase.classname
                Duration = [double]$testcase.time
                Status = "passed"
            }

            if ($testcase.failure) {
                $test.Status = "failed"
                $test.Message = $testcase.failure.message
                $results.Summary.Failed++
            }
            elseif ($testcase.skipped) {
                $test.Status = "skipped"
                $results.Summary.Skipped++
            }
            else {
                $results.Summary.Passed++
            }

            $results.Tests += $test
        }
    }

    return $results
}

function Get-JsonTestResults {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [string]$FilePath
    )

    if (-not (Test-Path $FilePath)) {
        return @{Tests = @(); TotalTime = 0; Summary = @{Passed = 0; Failed = 0; Skipped = 0}}
    }

    $json = Get-Content $FilePath | ConvertFrom-Json
    $results = @{Tests = @(); TotalTime = $json.duration; Summary = @{Passed = 0; Failed = 0; Skipped = 0}}

    foreach ($test in $json.tests) {
        $testObj = @{
            Name = $test.name
            Duration = $test.duration
            Status = $test.status
        }

        if ($test.status -eq "passed") {
            $results.Summary.Passed++
        }
        elseif ($test.status -eq "failed") {
            $results.Summary.Failed++
            $testObj.Message = $test.message
        }
        elseif ($test.status -eq "skipped") {
            $results.Summary.Skipped++
        }

        $results.Tests += $testObj
    }

    return $results
}

function Aggregate-TestResults {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [object[]]$TestResults = @()
    )

    $aggregated = @{
        Passed = 0
        Failed = 0
        Skipped = 0
        Duration = 0
        AllTests = @()
    }

    foreach ($result in $TestResults) {
        if ($result.Summary) {
            $aggregated.Passed += $result.Summary.Passed
            $aggregated.Failed += $result.Summary.Failed
            $aggregated.Skipped += $result.Summary.Skipped
        }
        $aggregated.Duration += $result.TotalTime
        $aggregated.AllTests += @($result.Tests)
    }

    return $aggregated
}

function ConvertTo-MarkdownSummary {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [object]$AggregatedResults
    )

    $markdown = @"
# Test Results Summary

## Totals

| Metric | Count |
|--------|-------|
| **Passed** | $($AggregatedResults.Passed) |
| **Failed** | $($AggregatedResults.Failed) |
| **Skipped** | $($AggregatedResults.Skipped) |
| **Total Duration** | $([math]::Round($AggregatedResults.Duration, 2))s |

"@

    return $markdown
}

function Find-FlakyTests {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [object[]]$MultipleRuns
    )

    $testStatuses = @{}

    foreach ($run in $MultipleRuns) {
        foreach ($test in $run.Tests) {
            if (-not $testStatuses[$test.Name]) {
                $testStatuses[$test.Name] = @()
            }
            $testStatuses[$test.Name] += $test.Status
        }
    }

    $flaky = @()
    foreach ($testName in $testStatuses.Keys) {
        $statuses = $testStatuses[$testName]
        if (($statuses -contains "passed") -and ($statuses -contains "failed")) {
            $flaky += , @{Name = $testName; Statuses = $statuses}
        }
    }

    return , $flaky
}

function Invoke-TestResultsAggregator {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [string[]]$TestResultFiles = @(),
        [string]$OutputFormat = "markdown"
    )

    # Placeholder implementation
    Write-Host "Test Results Aggregator initialized"
}

input.old_string:
name: Test Results Aggregator

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 0 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  actions: read

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Run Tests and Aggregate Results

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install PowerShell
        shell: bash
        run: |
          # Install PowerShell if not available
          if ! command -v pwsh &> /dev/null; then
            apt-get update
            apt-get install -y wget
            wget -q https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/releases/download/v7.4.1/powershell_7.4.1-1.deb_amd64.deb
            dpkg -i powershell_7.4.1-1.deb_amd64.deb
          fi
          pwsh --version

      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Run all tests in the repository
          $testResults = Invoke-Pester -Path "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1" -PassThru

          # Check if tests passed
          if ($testResults.FailedCount -gt 0) {
            Write-Host "::error::$($testResults.FailedCount) test(s) failed"
            exit 1
          }

          Write-Host "All $($testResults.PassedCount) tests passed successfully"

      - name: Test fixture parsing
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Source the aggregator script
          . ./test-results-aggregator.ps1

          # Test JUnit XML parsing
          $xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run1.xml"
          $xml2 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run2.xml"

          Write-Host "Run 1 - Passed: $($xml1.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($xml1.Summary.Failed), Skipped: $($xml1.Summary.Skipped)"
          Write-Host "Run 2 - Passed: $($xml2.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($xml2.Summary.Failed), Skipped: $($xml2.Summary.Skipped)"

          # Test JSON parsing
          $json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run1.json"
          $json2 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run2.json"

          Write-Host "JSON Run 1 - Passed: $($json1.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($json1.Summary.Failed)"
          Write-Host "JSON Run 2 - Passed: $($json2.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($json2.Summary.Failed)"

          # Identify flaky tests
          $flaky = Find-FlakyTests -MultipleRuns @($xml1, $xml2)
          if ($flaky) {
            Write-Host "Flaky tests detected:"
            foreach ($test in $flaky) {
              Write-Host "  - $($test.Name): $($test.Statuses -join ', ')"
            }
          }

      - name: Generate markdown summary
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Source the aggregator script
          . ./test-results-aggregator.ps1

          # Parse all test files
          $xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run1.xml"
          $xml2 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run2.xml"
          $json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run1.json"
          $json2 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run2.json"

          # Aggregate results
          $allResults = Aggregate-TestResults -TestResults @($xml1, $xml2, $json1, $json2)

          # Convert to markdown
          $markdown = ConvertTo-MarkdownSummary -AggregatedResults $allResults

          # Find flaky tests
          $flaky = Find-FlakyTests -MultipleRuns @($xml1, $xml2, $json1, $json2)

          # Append flaky tests section if any found
          if ($flaky) {
            $markdown += "`n## Flaky Tests`n`n"
            foreach ($test in $flaky) {
              $markdown += "- **$($test.Name)**: Inconsistent across runs`n"
            }
          }

          # Write to job summary
          $markdown | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY -Encoding utf8 -Append

          Write-Host "Summary written to GitHub job summary"
          Write-Host $markdown

  validate-workflow:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Validate Workflow Structure

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Verify script files exist
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $requiredFiles = @(
            "test-results-aggregator.ps1",
            "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1",
            "fixtures/junit-run1.xml",
            "fixtures/junit-run2.xml",
            "fixtures/results-run1.json",
            "fixtures/results-run2.json"
          )

          foreach ($file in $requiredFiles) {
            if (-not (Test-Path $file)) {
              Write-Host "::error::Required file missing: $file"
              exit 1
            }
            Write-Host "✓ $file exists"
          }

      - name: Validate YAML syntax
        shell: bash
        run: |
          # Check if actionlint is available
          if command -v actionlint &> /dev/null; then
            actionlint .github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml
          else
            echo "actionlint not found, skipping YAML validation"
          fi

name: Test Results Aggregator

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 0 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  actions: read

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Run Tests

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Run all tests in the repository
          $testResults = Invoke-Pester -Path "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1" -PassThru

          if ($testResults.FailedCount -gt 0) {
            Write-Host "::error::$($testResults.FailedCount) test(s) failed"
            exit 1
          }

          Write-Host "✓ All $($testResults.PassedCount) tests passed successfully"

      - name: Aggregate test results
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          . ./test-results-aggregator.ps1

          # Parse test result files
          $xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run1.xml"
          $xml2 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run2.xml"
          $json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run1.json"
          $json2 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run2.json"

          # Aggregate all results
          $allResults = Aggregate-TestResults -TestResults @($xml1, $xml2, $json1, $json2)

          # Generate markdown summary
          $markdown = ConvertTo-MarkdownSummary -AggregatedResults $allResults

          # Detect flaky tests
          $flaky = Find-FlakyTests -MultipleRuns @($xml1, $xml2, $json1, $json2)
          if ($flaky) {
            $markdown += "`n## Flaky Tests`n`n"
            foreach ($test in $flaky) {
              $markdown += "- **$($test.Name)**: Inconsistent across runs`n"
            }
          }

name: Test Results Aggregator

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 0 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  actions: read

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Run Tests and Aggregate Results

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install PowerShell
        shell: bash
        run: |
          # Install PowerShell if not available
          if ! command -v pwsh &> /dev/null; then
            apt-get update
            apt-get install -y wget
            wget -q https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/releases/download/v7.4.1/powershell_7.4.1-1.deb_amd64.deb
            dpkg -i powershell_7.4.1-1.deb_amd64.deb
          fi
          pwsh --version

      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Run all tests in the repository
          $testResults = Invoke-Pester -Path "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1" -PassThru

          # Check if tests passed
          if ($testResults.FailedCount -gt 0) {
            Write-Host "::error::$($testResults.FailedCount) test(s) failed"
            exit 1
          }

          Write-Host "All $($testResults.PassedCount) tests passed successfully"

      - name: Test fixture parsing
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Source the aggregator script
          . ./test-results-aggregator.ps1

          # Test JUnit XML parsing
          $xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run1.xml"
          $xml2 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run2.xml"

          Write-Host "Run 1 - Passed: $($xml1.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($xml1.Summary.Failed), Skipped: $($xml1.Summary.Skipped)"
          Write-Host "Run 2 - Passed: $($xml2.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($xml2.Summary.Failed), Skipped: $($xml2.Summary.Skipped)"

          # Test JSON parsing
          $json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run1.json"
          $json2 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run2.json"

          Write-Host "JSON Run 1 - Passed: $($json1.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($json1.Summary.Failed)"
          Write-Host "JSON Run 2 - Passed: $($json2.Summary.Passed), Failed: $($json2.Summary.Failed)"

          # Identify flaky tests
          $flaky = Find-FlakyTests -MultipleRuns @($xml1, $xml2)
          if ($flaky) {
            Write-Host "Flaky tests detected:"
            foreach ($test in $flaky) {
              Write-Host "  - $($test.Name): $($test.Statuses -join ', ')"
            }
          }

      - name: Generate markdown summary
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          # Source the aggregator script
          . ./test-results-aggregator.ps1

          # Parse all test files
          $xml1 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run1.xml"
          $xml2 = Get-JunitXmlTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/junit-run2.xml"
          $json1 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run1.json"
          $json2 = Get-JsonTestResults -FilePath "./fixtures/results-run2.json"

          # Aggregate results
          $allResults = Aggregate-TestResults -TestResults @($xml1, $xml2, $json1, $json2)

          # Convert to markdown
          $markdown = ConvertTo-MarkdownSummary -AggregatedResults $allResults

          # Find flaky tests
          $flaky = Find-FlakyTests -MultipleRuns @($xml1, $xml2, $json1, $json2)

          # Append flaky tests section if any found
          if ($flaky) {
            $markdown += "`n## Flaky Tests`n`n"
            foreach ($test in $flaky) {
              $markdown += "- **$($test.Name)**: Inconsistent across runs`n"
            }
          }

          # Write to job summary
          $markdown | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY -Encoding utf8 -Append

          Write-Host "Summary written to GitHub job summary"
          Write-Host $markdown

  validate-workflow:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: Validate Workflow Structure

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Verify script files exist
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $requiredFiles = @(
            "test-results-aggregator.ps1",
            "test-results-aggregator.Tests.ps1",
            "fixtures/junit-run1.xml",
            "fixtures/junit-run2.xml",
            "fixtures/results-run1.json",
            "fixtures/results-run2.json"
          )

          foreach ($file in $requiredFiles) {
            if (-not (Test-Path $file)) {
              Write-Host "::error::Required file missing: $file"
              exit 1
            }
            Write-Host "✓ $file exists"
          }

      - name: Validate YAML syntax
        shell: bash
        run: |
          # Check if actionlint is available
          if command -v actionlint &> /dev/null; then
            actionlint .github/workflows/test-results-aggregator.yml
          else
            echo "actionlint not found, skipping YAML validation"
          fi
